questions.q: In which Indian city was the film studio New Theatres founded?
questions.explanation: New Theatres was established in Calcutta, becoming the most prestigious studio of Bengali and Hindi cinema in the 1930s.

questions.q: Who founded the New Theatres studio?
questions.explanation: Birendra Nath Sircar, popularly known as B. N. Sircar, founded New Theatres in Calcutta.

questions.q: In which year was New Theatres founded?
questions.explanation: New Theatres was founded by B. N. Sircar in 1931 in Calcutta.

questions.q: Which legendary singer-actor was the leading star of New Theatres?
questions.explanation: Kundan Lal Saigal, India's first nationwide singing superstar, rose to fame through New Theatres productions.

questions.q: Which 1935 film made at New Theatres is considered one of the most influential adaptations of a Saratchandra Chattopadhyay novel?
questions.explanation: New Theatres produced the landmark 1935 version of Devdas, adapted from Saratchandra Chattopadhyay's novel.

questions.q: New Theatres was especially known for adapting which kind of source material into films?
questions.explanation: New Theatres earned its prestige by adapting Bengali and Hindi literary novels, particularly works by Saratchandra and Tagore.

questions.q: In which two languages did New Theatres famously produce bilingual versions of its films?
questions.explanation: New Theatres pioneered bilingual production, shooting many films in both Bengali and Hindi versions.

questions.q: Which acclaimed director associated with New Theatres directed the 1935 Bengali Devdas and played the title role?
questions.explanation: Pramathesh Chandra Barua directed the 1935 Bengali Devdas at New Theatres and starred as Devdas himself.

questions.q: New Theatres is most closely associated with the cinema of which Indian region in the 1930s?
questions.explanation: New Theatres was the flagship Bengal studio and the centre of prestige Bengali and Hindi cinema in the 1930s.

questions.q: Which influential music composer was a key figure at New Theatres?
questions.explanation: Rai Chand Boral, often called a pioneer of Indian film music, was the leading composer at New Theatres.

questions.q: In the 1935 Hindi version of Devdas produced by New Theatres, who played the role of Devdas?
questions.explanation: K. L. Saigal played Devdas in the 1935 Hindi version, while Barua played the role in the Bengali version.

questions.q: Which New Theatres composer, alongside R. C. Boral, helped pioneer playback singing in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Pankaj Mullick, a composer and singer at New Theatres, was instrumental in developing playback singing in Indian films.

questions.q: Director Bimal Roy began his film career as a cameraman and assistant at which studio?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy started his career as a cinematographer and assistant at New Theatres before becoming a celebrated director.

questions.q: Which 1937 New Theatres film, directed by P. C. Barua and featuring K. L. Saigal, became a major social drama success?
questions.explanation: Mukti (1937), directed by P. C. Barua, was a notable New Theatres social drama of the period.

questions.q: The famous song 'Babul Mora Naihar Chhuto Hi Jaye', sung by K. L. Saigal, featured in which 1938 New Theatres film?
questions.explanation: K. L. Saigal's celebrated rendition of 'Babul Mora' appeared in the New Theatres film Street Singer (1938).

questions.q: The 1935 New Theatres film Dhoop Chhaon (Bhagya Chakra in Bengali) is historically significant for being among the first Indian films to use what technique?
questions.explanation: Dhoop Chhaon is widely credited as one of the earliest Indian films to use the playback singing technique.

questions.q: Which director, known for socially conscious films like Chandidas, was a major creative force at New Theatres?
questions.explanation: Debaki Bose directed several acclaimed New Theatres films, including Chandidas, blending devotion and social reform themes.

questions.q: Which singer-actor, alongside K. L. Saigal, was a prominent leading man at New Theatres in films like Mukti and Dushman?
questions.explanation: Pahari Sanyal was a noted singer-actor at New Theatres, appearing in films such as Mukti (1937) and Dushman (1938).

questions.q: K. L. Saigal's nationally popular ghazals and songs at New Theatres were largely composed by which music director?
questions.explanation: R. C. Boral composed many of K. L. Saigal's most famous New Theatres songs that won him nationwide fame.

questions.q: Which 1934 New Theatres film directed by Debaki Bose, based on a Vaishnava poet's life, was a landmark devotional film?
questions.explanation: Chandidas (1934), directed by Debaki Bose at New Theatres, dramatised the life of the medieval Vaishnava poet.

questions.q: Which actress was a leading female star at New Theatres, appearing opposite K. L. Saigal in several films?
questions.explanation: Kanan Devi, one of Bengal's earliest singing stars, was a leading lady at New Theatres.

questions.q: The New Theatres studio operated on what kind of organisational model, similar to Bombay Talkies?
options: A salaried, contracted staff studio system
questions.explanation: New Theatres ran on the studio system with salaried, contracted directors, technicians and stars under one roof.

questions.q: Which 1937 New Theatres film, based on a Maithili poet's life and made in Hindi and Bengali, was directed by Debaki Bose?
questions.explanation: Vidyapati (1937), directed by Debaki Bose at New Theatres, depicted the life of the medieval poet Vidyapati.

questions.q: Director Nitin Bose, associated with New Theatres, is often credited with introducing which technical innovation to Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Nitin Bose is widely credited with introducing the playback singing method in Indian cinema through New Theatres' Dhoop Chhaon.

questions.q: Which New Theatres film of 1937, directed by Nitin Bose, was a notable bilingual social drama starring K. L. Saigal?
questions.explanation: President (1937), directed by Nitin Bose, was a New Theatres bilingual social drama featuring K. L. Saigal.

questions.q: After leaving New Theatres, K. L. Saigal moved to which city's film industry, where he made films like Tansen and Shahjehan?
questions.explanation: K. L. Saigal moved to Bombay in the early 1940s, where he made hits such as Tansen and Shahjehan before his death in 1947.

questions.q: Founder B. N. Sircar was also instrumental in establishing which film distribution and exhibition enterprise in Calcutta?
questions.explanation: B. N. Sircar built up exhibition and distribution interests, including International Filmcraft, alongside founding New Theatres.

questions.q: The decline of New Theatres in the 1940s was driven largely by which broader industry shift in Indian cinema?
options: The rise of regional silent cinema
The collapse of the studio system and rise of independent producers
A government ban on Bengali films
questions.explanation: Like other big studios, New Theatres declined in the 1940s as the studio system broke down and freelance independent producers rose.

questions.q: Which Tagore-related literary sensibility distinguished New Theatres' output from the action and stunt films of rival studios?
options: Refined, restrained literary and humanist storytelling
questions.explanation: New Theatres was known for refined, humanist literary storytelling, in contrast to the stunt and action films of many contemporaries.

questions.q: B. N. Sircar's contribution to Indian cinema was later recognised when he received which honour from the film industry?
questions.explanation: B. N. Sircar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award (in 1970) for his pioneering contribution to Indian cinema through New Theatres.
